data.title: QuantumScape Stock (QS) Drops Sharply After Earnings — Is the Selloff Overdone?
data.description:
QuantumScape (QS), the solid-state EV battery developer, fell sharply after its latest earnings report, which was released last week. The stock continued to trend higher in early trading on Friday after it rose about 0.2% on Thursday, ending a six-day losing streak. The bounce was small and came after heavy selling. The stock is now down roughly 32% year-to-date and about 30% over the past month.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earnings Beat Failed to Lift the Stock

The decline came after the company reported its quarterly results. QuantumScape reported a smaller-than-expected loss of $0.17 per share, slightly better than forecasts. However, the main concern remained unchanged, as the company still generated no meaningful revenue from its core battery business.

For now, the company is still in the development stage rather than a full operating business. It recorded about $19.5 million in customer billings last year and ended 2025 with roughly $971 million in cash. Management said its main focus is to prove the batteries can be produced at scale and used by automaker partners.

As a result, investors looked past the earnings beat and focused on the lack of actual product sales.

Wall Street's Take on QuantumScape

Analysts remain cautious on the stock, with all six covering analysts currently rating the shares Hold.

In a new research report, HSBC analyst Laisha Zaack upgraded the stock to Hold from Reduce with an $8.30 price target. She cited manufacturing progress, including the use of the faster Cobra production process, shipments of QSE-5 cells, installation of equipment for the Eagle production line, and new customer partnerships.

However, she still remains cautious. The company expects higher losses and has shared few details about its future plans, which has kept investor confidence weak.

Is QS Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on QuantumScape stock based on six unanimous Hold ratings assigned in the past three months. At $9.76, the average QS stock price target implies a 36.12% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Meta Platforms Trims Stock Awards as AI Investments Take Priority
data.description:
Social media giant Meta Platforms (META) has reduced its annual stock awards by about 5% for most of its employees, the Financial Times reported. The move comes as the company is prioritizing investments to support its artificial intelligence (AI) ambitions, including hiring top AI talent and building data centers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta Cuts Stock Options for Second Consecutive Year Amid AI Focus

The 5% reported cut marks the second consecutive year that Meta Platforms has slashed its annual stock option distribution. Last year, the company lowered its equity-based awards by about 10%.

The reduction in this key component of employee compensation comes as CEO Mark Zuckerberg is directing massive investments toward AI infrastructure as the race to develop advanced models intensifies, with players like Alphabet-owned Google (GOOGL) and ChatGPT-maker OpenAI giving tough competition.

Notably, Meta Platforms expects 2026 capital expenditures in the range of $115 billion to $135 billion, nearly double the $72.2 billion spent last year. Given the massive investments, the company is focusing on driving efficiency to mitigate the impact on its profitability. For instance, in January 2026, Meta slashed about 10% of the jobs in its Reality Labs division.

Is META a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Meta Platforms stock based on 40 Buys and four Hold recommendations. The average META stock price target of $856.11 indicates 33% upside potential. META stock is down 2% year-to-date.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Is Opendoor Stock (OPEN) Rallying Today?
data.description:
Shares of Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) stock are up 18% in pre-market trading despite reporting mixed results for the fiscal fourth quarter. The residential real estate platform posted a loss of $1.26 per share, significantly wider than analysts' estimated loss of $0.12. Revenue dropped 32% year-over-year to $736 million but exceeded expectations of $594 million.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/20/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Climb Ahead of Key Inflation Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose early Friday ahead of key Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index data for December, due this morning. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.35%, 0.24%, and 0.16%, respectively, at 5:29 a.m. EST on February 20.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major averages closed lower amid rising geopolitical tensions. The DJIA declined by 0.54%, while the S&P 500 dropped 0.28%, and the Nasdaq Composite Index fell 0.31%. Stocks declined yesterday, snapping a three-day winning streak for both the S&P 500 and the Dow, driven by oil prices reaching a six-month high amid escalating U.S.-Iran war tensions.

Turning to economic data, traders await key releases, including the fourth-quarter GDP, projected at 2.5% growth following Q3's stronger-than-expected 4.4% rise. Economists forecast a 2.8% annual gain in headline PCE and 3% in core PCE.

No key earnings releases are scheduled for today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.07%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $66.62 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $5,027 per ounce on Friday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as traders reviewed economic data and earnings while monitoring U.S.-Iran tensions.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded lower on February 20, while Mainland Chinese markets remained closed for the Lunar New Year.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 1.10%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei declined 1.12%, and the Topix lost 1.13%.

data.title: Kraken Parent Company Buys Token Management Firm Magna Ahead of IPO
data.description:
Payward, the parent company of crypto exchange Kraken, announced it has acquired token management platform Magna as part of its expansion strategy ahead of a potential initial public offering (IPO). The acquisition was disclosed on the exchange's official blog on Feb. 18. Terms of the transaction were not publicly disclosed. 

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Magna provides tooling for crypto projects to manage token vesting, claims, scheduled distributions, and related operational workflows across multiple blockchains. According to Payward, Magna has supported more than 160 clients and recorded a peak total value locked of $60 billion in 2025. The platform will continue to operate as a standalone service while teams begin integration work with Kraken's broader suite of institutional products. 

Kraken Expands Institutional Token Infrastructure with Magna

Payward framed the acquisition as a strategic step to build out a vertical stack of services beyond core trading infrastructure. Kraken has been adding products and services through acquisitions and internal development as it prepares for the transition to a public company. Magna's technology is expected to bolster token lifecycle management capabilities offered to project issuers and institutional clients, spanning early-stage fundraising through distribution and liquidity operations. 

Magna CEO Bruno Faviero said joining Kraken provides the company with access to institutional-grade infrastructure, deeper liquidity, and global distribution. The firm will initially focus on onboarding and foundational integrations before aligning products more directly with Kraken's roadmap. 

A Strategic Aquisition Ahead of IPO 

The acquisition of Magna continues Kraken's series of deals as it builds product breadth ahead of going public. In November 2025, Kraken confidentially filed for an IPO with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and subsequently released select 2025 financial results, a practice common among companies preparing to list. 

Market observers view expanding infrastructure and services as part of broader efforts to strengthen Kraken's institutional cryptocurrency offerings, in line with moves by other exchanges diversifying beyond spot trading. 

Is There a Kraken Stock?

Kraken's parent company, Payward, is privately held and not listed on public exchanges. Investors looking to follow developments or invest in its stock after the exchange's IPO can find additional details through TipRanks' private company center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Tesla 'Most Affordable' Cybertruck Still Above Musk's $40,000 Promise
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is expanding its Cybertruck lineup with a more affordable option for U.S. buyers. The company has opened orders for its "most affordable" Cybertruck yet, starting at $59,990. The company also cut the price of the high-performance Cyberbeast variant. The move signals Tesla's effort to boost demand, reach a broader customer base, and strengthen its competitive position in the electric pickup market.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla Opens Orders for $59,990 Cybertruck in US

Tesla has opened orders for its lowest-priced Cybertruck yet, with a starting price of $59,990. While this is lower than previous versions, it remains far above the $40,000 base price that CEO Elon Musk promised when the truck was first unveiled in 2019. The new model is cheaper than the $79,990 dual-motor all-wheel-drive version that was the base option last year.

At the same time, the company cut the price of the Cyberbeast — its most expensive Cybertruck model — to $99,990, down from $114,990.

Tesla's Efforts to Revive Demand

Tesla Cybertruck is struggling. In July 2025, the company reported that sales had declined to around 5,000 units per quarter, or approximately 20,000 vehicles per year. This is far below its earlier plan to build more than 250,000 trucks annually. The vehicle has also been hit with multiple recalls, including major safety concerns, and sales have been restricted in some countries because it does not meet local safety rules.

The latest price cut comes after a sharp drop in demand. Ongoing quality problems have weakened buyer confidence, while lower-priced rival models from Ford Motor (F) and General Motors (GM) have increased competitive pressure.

Price reductions are now a key part of Tesla's 2026 strategy, as the company lowers entry prices to attract more budget-conscious buyers instead of waiting to launch a brand-new mass-market vehicle.

Is Tesla a Buy or Sell Now?

According to TipRanks, TSLA stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Tesla shares is $396.80, suggesting a downside of 3.62% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Up Today, 2/20/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock market futures were higher ahead of Friday's session as investors awaited key economic data and a possible Supreme Court decision on President Donald Trump's tariffs. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.31%, 0.16%, and 0.27%, respectively, at 12:54 a.m. EST on February 20.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wall Street expects the court may decide whether the tariffs are legal under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. Traders think stocks could rise if the duties are struck down, even though the White House may later try other ways to reintroduce them.

On the economic front, traders will get fresh data with the release of fourth‑quarter GDP, which economists expect to show 2.5% growth after a stronger‑than‑expected 4.4% gain in Q3. Also, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, the Personal Consumption Expenditure, is due tomorrow. Estimates call for a 2.8% annual increase in headline PCE and a 3% rise in core PCE.

In the regular trading session, stocks fell, ending a three‑day winning streak for the S&P 500 and the Dow, as oil prices hit a six‑month high amid rising U.S.–Iran tensions. The Dow dropped 0.5%, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq each slipped 0.3%.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Gears Up for a Potential $20B Sale of Orthopedics Unit
data.description:
Healthcare giant Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) is gearing up to sell its orthopedics unit, with several large buyout firms already showing interest, Bloomberg reported. The company previously announced plans to separate its Orthopedics unit, DePuy Synthes, in October 2025. The business could reportedly be valued at over $20 billion.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

JNJ Prepares to Offload Orthopedics Business

Aside from big buyout firms, JNJ's DePuy Synthes business could also receive offers from rival medical device makers. The company's orthopedics unit, which manufactures devices used in hip and knee replacements, generated $9.3 billion in sales in 2025.

The Bloomberg report stated that talks are at an early stage and there is no guarantee that they will result in a sale of the orthopedics unit.

Back in October 2025, JNJ said that it intends to separate its slower-growing DePuy Synthes unit from the rest of the company within 18 to 24 months. CFO Joseph Wolk said the company was exploring several separation options, mainly focusing on a tax-free spinoff. However, he added that the company is open to ideas that others might have. Wolk said the separation process was already ongoing and material updates on the matter are not expected until mid-2026.

JNJ is facing multiple challenges, including tariff woes, legal troubles related to its talc products, and rising competition for key drugs from biosimilars. Meanwhile, on Wednesday, the company announced plans to invest more than $1 billion in a manufacturing facility in Pennsylvania for advanced cell therapy technologies.

Is JNJ a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Johnson & Johnson stock based on nine Buys and four Holds. The average JNJ stock price target of $243 indicates 1.6% downside risk from current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
